invariants.name: Exactly five primary commands in help
invariants.statement: `hawking --help` lists exactly {gravity, condense, doctor, serve, status} as subcommands and nothing else. No clap-hidden commands (grep for #[command(hide)] / #[arg(hide)] on top-level must be zero). Every historical verb resolves only via an alias shim that is NOT listed as a first-class subcommand.
invariants.test: Parse --help output; assert the subcommand set equals the 5; assert `hawking generate --help` prints the deprecation shim, not a peer command.

invariants.name: Plain hawking == hawking status
invariants.statement: Invoking the binary with no subcommand runs `status` and is byte-identical to `hawking status` (same schema, same read-only behavior, same exit code).
invariants.test: Diff stdout/JSON of `hawking` vs `hawking status`; assert equal and exit 0 with no lease acquisition.

invariants.name: Gravity CHOOSES bpw / Condense OBEYS bpw
invariants.statement: `gravity <model>` selects effective_bpw from evidence (output.chosen.effective_bpw is derived, not an input) and, given only --target-bpw as a ceiling, never exceeds it. `condense <model> <bpw>` produces an artifact whose measured achieved_bpw == the requested <bpw> within the byte-ledger tolerance, and REFUSES if it cannot hit it (no silent drift).
invariants.test: gravity --explain on a fixture: chosen bpw present, <= --target-bpw when set. condense with <bpw>=2: assert achieved_bpw==2±ledger_eps; condense with an unreachable bpw: assert non-zero exit + refusal, no artifact written.

invariants.name: Doctor read-only by default + independent verification path
invariants.statement: `doctor <target>` and `doctor --verify` perform ZERO mutation of the target, the campaign, or the lease (verified by pre/post sha256 of target + campaign root). --verify runs a code path that does NOT reuse the producer (gravity/condense) code, so it can catch producer bugs. Only `doctor --treat` mutates, and only when authorized.
invariants.test: Snapshot target+campaign sha before/after doctor and doctor --verify; assert unchanged and no flock acquired. Assert --verify imports the verifier module, not the packer. Assert --treat without authorization refuses.

invariants.name: Serve verifies before load + exactly one endpoint
invariants.statement: `serve <artifact>` runs the artifact proof (ledger + .tq strict/all-linear/gpu as applicable) BEFORE binding the socket or loading weights; a FAIL exits non-zero with the port never bound and the model never loaded. Starting serve when the addr is already bound refuses (no duplicate /v1 endpoint, no second server binary).
invariants.test: Corrupt an artifact's ledger -> serve exits non-zero, `lsof` shows no bind, RSS shows no model load. Start two serves on the same addr -> second refuses. Assert only one code path owns /v1 (grep for a single router registration).

invariants.name: Status is not a shadow controller
invariants.statement: `status` (and bare `hawking`) can NEVER acquire-and-hold the lease, spawn/kill the controller, or mutate durable state. It decides liveness by a non-blocking flock probe that it immediately releases, and recounts progress from durable checkpoints (a stale cursor/PID cannot flip RUNNING). status exposes no --resume/--drain.
invariants.test: Run status against a live-lease fixture: assert it never wins-and-holds the flock (instrument SingletonLease), never sends signals, exit 0. Assert clap rejects `status --resume` and `status --drain`. Kill the holder while status --watch runs: state flips to NOT_STARTED/FAILED from flock truth, not from the stale PID/cursor.

invariants.name: Aliases warn and never spawn a duplicate controller
invariants.statement: Every deprecated verb (generate, bench, fit, press, verify, seed-c run/resume/drain, succ gravity-*, etc.) prints a deprecation warning to stderr, forwards to its five-command target, and — for anything that could drive the campaign — routes through the SAME singleton lease so no second controller can start. Two concurrent ignition attempts (any spelling) result in exactly one live controller.
invariants.test: For each alias in the migration_ledger, assert stderr warning + correct forwarded exit behavior. Concurrently invoke `gravity --resume` and `second_light_controller.py resume` on the same root: assert exactly one holds the flock and the other refuses cleanly (no corruption, no second PID).

invariants.name: No distributed/multi-machine surface
invariants.statement: None of the five commands, and no surviving alias, exposes workers/scheduling/plist/telegram/arm-* as a command. Persistence and notifications are automatic/config behaviors only.
invariants.test: grep the resolved command graph for worker/plist/arm-template/telegram command nodes -> zero. Assert watch-plist/arm-* have no alias entry that resolves to a runnable command.

invariants.name: Lifecycle flags mean the same thing everywhere
invariants.statement: --verify is always read-only; --explain always computes-without-side-effects; --drain always seals-and-releases (never hard-kills); --resume always re-enters at the last sealed checkpoint; --json always emits a schema-tagged object; on read-only commands the mutating flags are absent.
invariants.test: Property test across commands: with --explain no files/lease change; with --verify no mutation; --drain leaves a resumable sealed state (a subsequent --resume completes); every --json output validates against its declared schema id.

note: maps to the goal's required public-surface/gravity/condense/doctor/serve/status/migration tests
